$comment: Executable MVP gate manifest for S16A/S16B. It intentionally mirrors the SPECv2 §24.6 benchmark set from core-suite.json so the scenario wiring and the later release gate run against the same canonical scenario IDs in a stable order.

scenarios.name: Single clean issue with no blockers
scenarios.description: A single well-defined task issue with no scope overlap, no ambiguity, and no merge conflict. The happy-path baseline scenario. Aegis must dispatch, complete, and merge without any human intervention.

scenarios.name: Issue Oracle marks complex — system pauses correctly
scenarios.description: An issue that Oracle assesses as complex (complexity >= complex threshold). Aegis must pause the issue rather than dispatching Titan, and surface the pause to the operator. No autonomous continuation.

scenarios.name: Issue requiring decomposition into child issues
scenarios.description: An issue that Oracle decomposes into multiple child issues. Aegis must create the derived child issues and process them independently. The parent issue resolves once children complete.

scenarios.name: Titan ambiguity that must create a clarification issue
scenarios.description: An ambiguous issue where Titan cannot proceed safely. Aegis must create a clarification child issue linked to the original, preserve the labor for resumption, and leave an explicit handoff note. Titan must not improvise.

scenarios.name: Stale-branch merge requiring rework
scenarios.description: A merge candidate whose base branch has diverged since Titan's labor began, triggering rework before the merge can land. Tests the rework loop path without Janus involvement.

scenarios.name: Hard merge conflict with preserved labor
scenarios.description: A merge candidate that produces a conflict the deterministic queue cannot resolve. The labor must be preserved, a REWORK_REQUEST artifact emitted, and the issue surfaced for operator action.

scenarios.name: Tier 3 integration escalation — Janus resolves and requeues
scenarios.description: A Tier 3 conflict where Janus is permitted. Janus must produce a structured integration-resolution artifact, prepare a refreshed candidate, and return control to the merge queue. Janus must not merge directly.

scenarios.name: Janus semantic ambiguity — human-decision artifact required
scenarios.description: A Janus case where semantic ambiguity forces a human-decision artifact rather than unsafe auto-resolution. Janus must not bluff through the conflict; it must emit the human-decision artifact and stop.

scenarios.name: Orchestrator restart during Titan implementation
scenarios.description: The orchestrator is restarted while Titan is actively implementing. On recovery, Aegis must reconcile the in-progress dispatch state and resume without losing work or creating duplicate issues.

scenarios.name: Orchestrator restart during merge processing
scenarios.description: The orchestrator is restarted while the merge queue is processing a candidate. On recovery, the merge queue must reconcile its state and complete the merge safely without bypassing gates or duplicating the merge.

scenarios.name: Hooks disabled — polling remains the correctness path
scenarios.description: All Beads webhook integrations are disabled. Aegis must operate correctly using polling alone: issue discovery, status updates, and merge queue progression must all work without hook-driven events.
